title: Maus-Tec Language Pack
description: A language pack plugin for use with mt-i18n. Contains plugin metadata and a key-value translation dictionary keyed on symbolic i18n keys. Consumed by device firmware and validated by MausHub on upload.

name.description: Unique slug identifier for the language pack plugin. Used as a stable ID across versions.

version.description: Language pack version in semver format.

displayName.description: Human-readable pack name shown in the device UI and on MausHub.

description.description: Optional short description of the pack, shown on MausHub.

repository.description: URL to the source repository or contribution page.

type.description: Plugin type discriminator. Must be 'language' for all language packs.

language.description: Full human-readable name of the language, written in that language.

schema_version.description: Version of the firmware i18n key catalog this pack was authored against. Used by MausHub to compute translation completeness and to detect stale packs. Must match the schema_version in the firmware's emitted i18n-catalog.json build artifact.

fw_compat.description: Advisory firmware compatibility range. Not a hard install gate — missing or mismatched keys fall back to English at runtime. Used by Hub for informational display only.

min.description: Minimum firmware version this pack was tested with. Null means no lower bound.

max.description: Maximum firmware version this pack is known to work with. Null means no upper bound.

keys.description: Translation dictionary. Each property name is a dot-namespaced symbolic key (e.g. 'ui.back', 'toast.saved'); the value is the translated string in the target language. An empty string value ('') means 'use the English fallback for this key'. Format string specifiers (%s, %d, etc.) must be preserved exactly as they appear in the firmware's fallback string.

keyNamingNote.description: Keys use dot-separated namespaces. Defined namespaces: 'ui.*' for shared UI chrome (back, enter, save, stop); 'menu.*' for menu item labels; 'toast.*' for toast notifications; 'config.*' for config field labels; 'status.*' for device status strings. New namespaces may be added by firmware without a schema version bump — the firmware's i18n-catalog.json build artifact is the authoritative key list for a given firmware version.
